title: Why Joby Aviation Stock Soared 11.3% Last Month and Is Rocketing Higher in May
text: Joby Aviation stock performance has been driven by multiple positive catalysts and market developments. Joby reported progress toward commercial electric air taxi operations, highlighting new U.S. demonstration opportunities, certification milestones, manufacturing expansion and a stronger cash position following a recent capital raise. The company was selected for the White House-backed eIPP program across 11 states and expects agreements to start being signed in the third quarter, with operations potentially beginning in the second half of the year. Recent demonstration flights in New York and the Bay Area showed key operational capability, including flights between JFK and Manhattan heliports. Joby ended Q1 with about $2.5 billion in cash after recent capital raises, while continuing to ramp manufacturing, advance FAA certification, and report a smaller net loss than the prior quarter. Management reaffirmed its 2026 revenue outlook of $105–$115 million. Recent weeks have seen JOBY run from the high-$8s to above $11, with a close around $11.06 on May 13, 2026, representing a strong move of roughly 25 percent from late-April levels driven by headlines. The stock performance reflects investor enthusiasm for the company's progress toward commercialization.
